senses.definition: Opposing or reducing the effect of free radicals in the body or in chemical reactions.
examples: These compounds show strong antiradicals activity in laboratory tests.
The study compared the antiradicals properties of several plant extracts.

senses.definition: A person who strongly opposes the building or use of railways.
examples: Local antirailwayists protested against the new high-speed line.
He was known as an outspoken antirailwayist in the nineteenth century.

senses.definition: Opposing railways or railway expansion.
examples: Antirailwayist views slowed the project for several years.
The pamphlet spread antirailwayist ideas across the region.

senses.definition: Opposing rape or aiming to prevent rape.
examples: She volunteers with an antirape organization.
The government funded several antirape education programs in schools.

senses.definition: Opposed to rational thought or logical reasoning.
examples: The movement took an openly antirational stance on science and progress.
Critics described his antirational approach to policy as dangerous.

senses.definition: The belief or attitude that rejects rational thought as the main way to understand the world.
examples: Antirationalism gained strength as people lost trust in scientific experts.
The book traces the rise of antirationalism in modern culture.

senses.definition: Plural of antirationalism; different forms or examples of rejecting rational thought.
examples: The author compares various antirationalisms in twentieth-century politics.
Religious and political antirationalisms often feed each other.

senses.definition: A person who rejects rational thought as the main way to understand the world.
examples: He was labelled an antirationalist for his attacks on science.
Many antirationalists distrust modern technology and expertise.

senses.definition: Showing or related to opposition to rational thought.
examples: The text offers an antirationalist critique of Enlightenment values.
Her antirationalist position appeals to those who feel excluded by experts.

senses.definition: Having the quality of opposing or rejecting rational ways of thinking.
examples: They promoted antirationalistic ideas in their writings.
The play has a strongly antirationalistic tone.

senses.definition: Plural of antirationalist; people who reject rational thought as central.
examples: Antirationalists argue that emotion and faith matter more than logic.
The conference brought together antirationalists from several traditions.

senses.definition: Plural of antirationality; different ways in which thought or behavior goes against rational thinking.
examples: The paper explores the various antirationalities present in extremist groups.
These social antirationalities are hard to correct with facts alone.

senses.definition: The quality of being against or outside rational thinking.
examples: He warned about growing antirationality in public debate.
The author links antirationality to fear and insecurity.

senses.definition: In a way that goes against rational thought.
examples: They reacted antirationally to the expert advice.
The policy was antirationally designed, ignoring basic data.

senses.definition: A device or material used to stop objects from rattling.
examples: They fitted an antirattler inside the door panel.
An antirattler can reduce noise from loose parts in the car.

senses.definition: Designed to prevent or reduce a chemical or physical reaction.
examples: The antireacting coating protects the metal from corrosion.
An antireacting layer was added to stop the materials from bonding.

senses.definition: Opposition to a political or social reaction, especially to conservative or backward changes.
examples: The party formed an antireaction front against the new regime.
Her article called for antireaction to growing nationalism.

senses.definition: Plural of antireactionary; people who oppose strongly conservative or backward political forces.
examples: Antireactionaries organized protests across the country.
The book describes clashes between reactionaries and antireactionaries.

senses.definition: Opposing strongly conservative or backward political attitudes or policies.
examples: The group adopted an antireactionary platform.
Antireactionary forces pushed for democratic reforms.

senses.definition: A person who opposes strongly conservative or backward political forces.
examples: As an antireactionary, she supported the student protests.
The antireactionaries formed a new coalition party.

senses.definition: Designed to prevent, limit, or show no chemical reaction.
examples: An antireactive layer separates the two chemicals.
The container is made from antireactive material.

senses.definition: In philosophy, the view that we should not think of certain things, like moral values or unobservable entities, as existing independently of our minds or language.
examples: Antirealism challenges the idea that scientific theories describe a mind-independent world.
Her work on moral antirealism argues that there are no objective moral facts.

senses.definition: Plural of antirealism; different types or versions of antirealist views in philosophy.
examples: The article surveys the main antirealisms in contemporary metaphysics.
These antirealisms differ in how they treat truth and reference.

senses.definition: A philosopher or thinker who holds an antirealist view.
examples: An antirealist might deny that numbers exist independently of our theories.
Moral antirealists reject objective moral facts.

senses.definition: Relating to or supporting antirealism.
examples: She offers an antirealist interpretation of quantum theory.
Their antirealist position stresses the role of language in shaping reality.

senses.definition: Not accepting or going against a realist view, especially in philosophy or art.
examples: The novel takes an antirealistic approach to time and identity.
His antirealistic stance in ethics rejects objective values.

senses.definition: In a way that rejects a realist view or realistic style.
examples: The story unfolds antirealistically, with sudden jumps in time.
He writes antirealistically about moral questions.

senses.definition: Plural of antirealist; people who support antirealist positions.
examples: Antirealists argue that truth depends on what can be proved or justified.
Scientific antirealists remain agnostic about unobservable entities.

senses.definition: Opposition to or rejection of what is usually accepted as reality.
examples: The film creates a strange sense of antireality.
Their propaganda spreads an atmosphere of antireality and confusion.

senses.definition: Intended to prevent or restrict the practice of giving secret or unfair rebates.
examples: Antirebating laws were introduced to protect small competitors.
The company changed its policies to comply with antirebating regulations.

senses.definition: Intended to prevent or lessen an economic recession.
examples: The government announced a series of antirecession measures.
Antirecession policies focused on boosting consumer spending.

senses.definition: Intended to prevent or reduce the harmful effects of an economic recession.
examples: Past antirecessionary programs have taken effect slowly.
The central bank may support antirecessionary fiscal actions.

senses.definition: Efforts or measures aimed at preventing economic recessions.
examples: Different antirecessions were tried during the crisis.
These antirecessions relied heavily on public spending.

senses.definition: Opposing or trying to stop the recruitment of people, especially into the military or certain organizations.
examples: Students organized an antirecruiting protest on campus.
Antirecruiting campaigns questioned the fairness of military service.

senses.definition: Opposed to communist or left-wing ideas, especially during the Cold War period.
examples: The senator was known for his antired speeches.
Antired feelings were strong in the early 1950s.

senses.definition: The prevention of particles, dirt, or material from being deposited again on a surface after removal.
examples: The detergent contains agents for antiredeposition of soil on fabrics.
Antiredeposition is important in modern cleaning products.

senses.definition: A substance or device that prevents or limits reduction reactions, or a person opposing cost or size reductions.
examples: The additive works as an antireducer in the chemical process.
Antireducers in the company resisted further budget cuts.

senses.definition: Preventing or limiting a reduction process, either chemical or figurative.
examples: The antireducing environment slows the reaction.
Antireducing forces inside the firm resisted layoffs.

senses.definition: Opposition to reducing something, such as costs, staff, or complex ideas.
examples: Antireduction in public services became a key political issue.
There is a strong antireduction feeling among employees.

senses.definition: The view that complex things, such as the mind or society, cannot be fully explained just by their simplest parts.
examples: Antireductionism in philosophy of mind stresses the importance of higher-level properties.
Her antireductionism rejects attempts to explain culture only through biology.

senses.definition: Plural of antireductionism; different forms of opposition to reductionist explanations.
examples: The paper compares several antireductionisms in contemporary biology.
These antireductionisms vary in how strongly they reject physical explanations.

senses.definition: A person who opposes reductionist explanations of complex systems.
examples: As an antireductionist, she argues that consciousness cannot be reduced to brain states.
Antireductionists emphasize the role of social structures.

senses.definition: Opposing reductionist explanations of complex systems.
examples: Their antireductionist view stresses emergent properties.
He defends an antireductionist approach to social science.

senses.definition: Plural of antireductionist; people who reject reductionist explanations.
examples: Antireductionists in biology argue for the importance of systems.
The debate between reductionists and antireductionists remains lively.

senses.definition: Opposing the idea of explaining complex things only in terms of their simplest parts.
examples: Her antireductive strategy highlights the complexity of mental states.
Antireductive arguments appear throughout the book.

senses.definition: The reduction or prevention of unwanted reflection from a surface, especially optical surfaces.
examples: The lenses have an antireflection coating.
Antireflection is important in high-quality camera systems.

senses.definition: Designed to reduce the amount of light that reflects from a surface.
examples: She bought glasses with an antireflective coating.
Antireflective screens are easier to use in bright light.

senses.definition: In mathematics or logic, describing a relation where no element is related to itself.
examples: The relation "is greater than" is antireflexive.
An antireflexive relation has an empty diagonal in its matrix form.

senses.definition: Opposed to proposed reforms or changes.
examples: Antireform groups blocked the new education bill.
He led an antireform campaign against welfare changes.

senses.definition: Opposition to reform.
examples: Antireform was strong among older members of the party.
The leader paid a price for his antireform stance.

senses.definition: A person who opposes proposed reforms or changes.
examples: Antireformers in parliament delayed the bill.
She became a leading antireformer in the debate over healthcare.

senses.definition: Opposing or blocking reforms.
examples: Antireforming voices grew louder after the election.
The bill faced antireforming criticism from conservative groups.

senses.definition: Opposed to reform and supportive of keeping existing systems.
examples: Antireformist elements in the party resisted any change.
The article criticizes the government's antireformist approach.

senses.definition: A person who opposes reforms.
examples: Antireformists blocked the proposal in the upper house.
He has long been an antireformist on constitutional issues.

senses.definition: Opposed to government rules and controls, especially on business.
examples: The party took an antiregulatory position on environmental laws.
Antiregulatory policies can weaken consumer protections.

senses.definition: Used to prevent the body from rejecting a transplanted organ or tissue.
examples: She was given antirejection drugs after the transplant.
Antirejection medicine must be taken for many years.

senses.definition: Strong opposition to religion or to religious beliefs and practices.
examples: Some critics accused the movement of antireligion.
Her book was read as a manifesto of antireligion.

senses.definition: Opposed to religion or religious influence.
examples: They criticized the government's antireligion policies.
Antireligion messages appeared in the campaign.
